name: init-design-review
description: Multi-agent design review for the /tf init redesign. Runs analyst + reviewer + risk-reviewer in parallel against docs/init-redesign-plan.md.

phases.task:
Review the design plan at /Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow/docs/init-redesign-plan.md. Read it fully.

Context: pi-taskflow, a Pi coding agent extension. The plan redesigns /tf init. Current code: extensions/index.ts lines 277-320 (tool path) and 570-650 (slash command). Model roles are resolved in extensions/agents.ts line 168. Reference module: extensions/runs-view.ts.

Your job: find AMBIGUITIES and MISSING REQUIREMENTS only. Other reviewers cover architecture and risk. Specifically:

1. Sections 1-3 imply requirements not stated. List each: stale role keys not in INIT_ROLES (section 7 says out of scope; verify section 3.2.a handles them), what if settings.json has no modelRoles key vs empty object, what if the user cancels mid-loop after editing some roles, etc.

2. For each open question Q1-Q5 in section 8, pick one side and justify briefly. Don't just list options.

3. Test plan in section 6 vs public API in section 4. Is every new code path covered? Are there tests for failure modes in the risk table (R1, R4, R5, R10)?

4. User-visible behavior changes not called out in section 4.

Output as markdown with these sections (no other prose):
## Ambiguities / missing requirements
(each: severity [blocker|major|minor], location, description, suggested fix)

## Open question verdicts
(one short paragraph per Q1-Q5: pick, justify)

## Test coverage gaps
(section #, what's missing, suggested test name)

## Silent behavior changes
(old, new, documented where, flag in README?)

## Verdict
(PASS | NEEDS_REVISION | BLOCK, 1-2 sentences)

Quote line/section numbers. Findings only, no plan restatement.

Review the architecture and API design in /Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow/docs/init-redesign-plan.md. Read it fully.

Context: pi-taskflow extension. Current code: extensions/index.ts (709 lines, 2 duplicated init paths), extensions/agents.ts, extensions/runs-view.ts (~144 line class with handleInput/render/invalidate — a clean extracted module). Test runner: `node --experimental-strip-types --test`. Pi UI primitives in node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts ExtensionUIContext around line 67: select, input, confirm, notify, custom.

Your job: review MODULE BOUNDARIES, SIGNATURES, IDIOMATIC PATTERNS. Other reviewers cover requirements and risk.

Specifically:
1. Section 3.1 init.ts exports — right boundaries? Class vs functions? Multiple files? Compare to runs-view.ts (class-based) — follow or diverge?

2. InitFlowResult with kind: 'saved'|'cancelled'|'no-change' — right shape? Discriminated union / builder pattern better?

3. Tool schema mode: 'show'|'apply-defaults'|'interactive' — right vocabulary? Add 'reset'? Reject unknown modes at typebox level or runtime?

4. buildRoleOptions(role, available, current, recommended) — clean or single context object? Footer 'Custom…'+'Keep current'+'Back' misleading when any is missing (first run, no current)?

5. Action menu as FIRST dialog — should first-run (no current) short-circuit to 'Use recommended defaults' without menu? Compare to current code lines 570-650.

6. INIT_ROLES + RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS as separate constants — cleaner as InitRole.defaultModel field (single source of truth)?

Output as markdown with:
## Architecture findings
(severity, location, description, fix)

## API surface findings
(severity, old, new, rationale)

## Idiomatic patterns
(pattern in plan, better alternative, where to apply)

## Verdict
(PASS | NEEDS_REVISION | BLOCK, 1-2 sentences)

Quote line/section numbers. Findings only.

Review the risk analysis in /Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow/docs/init-redesign-plan.md. Read it fully.

Context: pi-taskflow. Current action='init' at extensions/index.ts:277-320 writes ~/.pi/agent/settings.json via fs.writeFileSync (line 318). Loaded with JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(...)) (line 281). New plan must preserve non-modelRoles keys (e.g. subagents.agentOverrides).

Your job: review RISK TABLE R1-R10. Other reviewers cover requirements and architecture.

Specifically validate:
1. R1 (settings.json corruption) — atomic write (write to .tmp, rename) sound on macOS/Linux/Windows? Crash mid-write? Cross-filesystem rename failure?

2. R2 (breaking change) — 'show by default' strictly safer? Could a user relying on overwrite (LLM agent re-init) be broken? Deprecation warning / config opt-in / one-time migration?

3. R4 (custom input validation) — custom provider (self-hosted) not in registry — fallback? 'No modelRegistry, no validation' handled?

4. R5 (Esc undefined) — Esc at ACTION MENU vs custom input — does plan distinguish? runInteractiveInit signature in section 3.1 — way to know which dialog returned undefined?

5. R6 (test mocks diverge) — real ctx.ui API regression (new required field) not caught by mocks. Need e2e smoke in test/e2e.mts?

6. R7 (non-interactive/RPC) — ctx.hasUI branch — extensions/index.ts:513 (runs subcommand) pattern. New flow handle 'show report + offer schedule interactive init', or just 'go run /tf init'?

7. R8 (defaults stale) — 'Out of scope' right call, or warn if modelRegistry.find() can't resolve a default at init time?

8. R10 (Windows rename) — 'Best-effort, fall back to non-atomic write with warning' — implementable with fs.renameSync? Warning surfaced (some branches use ctx.ui.notify not console)?

Add new risks you spot (same row format).

Output as markdown with:
## Risk table validation
(R1-R10: agree|disagree + 1-2 sentences)

## New risks
(id, likelihood, impact, mitigation)

## Q1 verdict
(tool-path default behavior change IS a risk; take a position)

## Verdict
(PASS | NEEDS_REVISION | BLOCK, 1-2 sentences)

Quote section numbers. Findings only.

Synthesize the three review reports above into a single verdict for the design plan at /Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow/docs/init-redesign-plan.md. Focus on:

1. Are the three verdicts (PASS / NEEDS_REVISION / BLOCK) consistent? If they disagree, identify the root cause and pick the binding one.

2. Extract the top 5 most important ACTIONABLE findings across all three reports (deduplicated, ranked by impact).

3. Identify any findings that ALL THREE reviewers independently raised — these are the highest-confidence issues.

4. List 2-3 findings that the plan should explicitly add or change before implementation begins.

Output as markdown with:
## Cross-reviewer agreement
(any finding raised by 2+ reviewers)

## Top 5 actionable items
(ranked, with source reviewer)

## Disagreements
(reviewer A says X, reviewer B says Y, resolution)

## Final verdict
(PASS | NEEDS_REVISION | BLOCK, 1-2 sentences with the binding blocker if any)

Do not introduce new findings — only synthesize what the three reviewers said.
